---
title: Setup
description: Configure createCache with Redis, TTL overrides, and build-phase behavior.
---

## Basic setup

```ts title="lib/cache.ts"
import { createCache } from "@g14o/cache";
import { logger } from "@/lib/logger";
import { env } from "@/lib/env";

export const { withCache, invalidateCache, invalidateCacheKey } = createCache({
  redis: {
    url: env.UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL,
    token: env.UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN,
  },
  logger,
});
```

## Custom TTL

```ts title="lib/cache.ts"
export const { withCache, getTTL } = createCache({
  redis: {
    url: env.UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL,
    token: env.UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN,
  },
  ttl: {
    development: { short: 30, long: 900 },
    production: { medium: 3600 },
  },
});
```

## Shared Redis client

Reuse an existing `@upstash/redis` client across your app:

```ts title="lib/redis.ts"
import { Redis } from "@upstash/redis";

export const redis = Redis.fromEnv();
```

```ts title="lib/cache.ts"
import { createCache } from "@g14o/cache";
import { redis } from "@/lib/redis";
import { logger } from "@/lib/logger";

export const { withCache, invalidateCache, invalidateCacheKey } = createCache({ redis, logger });
```

## Build vs runtime

By default, `inMemoryDuringBuild` is `true`: during static build phases (Next.js sets `NEXT_PHASE` to `phase-production-build` or `phase-export` during `next build` / export), cache uses an in-memory adapter so prerender does not call Upstash. At runtime in production, Redis is used when configured.

To opt into Redis during builds (debugging only):

```ts
createCache({
  redis: { url: "...", token: "..." },
  inMemoryDuringBuild: false,
});
```

Import `isBuildLikePhase()` from `@g14o/cache/config` to branch on build phase in your own code.
